value.author: codex
value.text: 2026-05-29 release-readiness refresh: package manifest/lock now target @unbrained/pm-cli >=2026.5.29 / ^2026.5.29 and pm-changelog ^2026.5.29-2 where applicable. Verified npm run check, npm test, npm run pack:dry-run, npm run audit:prod, npm run changelog:full, npm run changelog:check, npm run release:check, and pm validate. High-confidence worktree/history secret scan recorded no credential-shaped token findings for active package code; nonzero scan rows were classified as placeholders or vendored historical test data where applicable. Logs: /tmp/pm-active-package-gates-20260529.tsv, /tmp/pm-active-changelog-20260529.tsv, /tmp/pm-active-release-check-20260529.tsv, /tmp/pm-active-validate-20260529.tsv, /tmp/pm-active-secret-scan-20260529.tsv.

value.author: codex
value.text: 2026-05-29 CI follow-up: investigated the red main CI run after the release-readiness refresh and confirmed the only failing gate was npm run changelog:check under a fresh clone with the complete remote tag set. Fetched origin tags locally, regenerated CHANGELOG.md with pm-changelog, verified npm run changelog:full, npm run changelog:check, and npm run release:check all pass against the corrected full-history changelog. Fast-forwarded local main to origin/main commit 17c34a7, which contains the matching generated changelog, so the next non-skipped package tracking commit will trigger CI on the corrected tree.

patch.value: pm-web uses the current pm CLI and SDK package expectations, generated CHANGELOG.md matches a complete remote-tag history, release-readiness checks pass locally, package tracking records the verification evidence, and the follow-up push gives GitHub CI a clean run on the corrected tree.

value.note: Package identity,release scripts,pm CLI/SDK dependency policy,and npm pack metadata.
Generated by pm-changelog with complete release-tag history.
Production-readiness audit record and evidence trail.

patch.value: pm-web was refreshed for the current pm package release line, the full-tag changelog mismatch was corrected upstream, local release checks pass on the corrected tree, and the follow-up tracking commit will exercise GitHub CI without skip markers.

pm-web remains installable as a pm package, generated changelog output is deterministic in fresh clones with all tags, local release checks pass, pm validation succeeds with only pre-existing tracker hygiene warnings, and GitHub CI has a non-skipped run to validate the corrected tree.

npm run release:check passed after fetching full remote tags and fast-forwarding to the corrected changelog; pm --path .agents/pm --json validate returned ok=true with known tracker hygiene warnings; the audit evidence is recorded on pm-web-8u17 for the non-skipped CI-triggering commit.
Release-readiness audit completed with local verification and CI follow-up prepared
